summary: Improved mnmain decompilation readability and type accuracy by replacing generic int placeholders with HSD_JObj* where the code is manipulating joint objects, simplifying the local menu user-data struct to a contiguous x4[42] JObj array, and renaming several locals in mn_8022B3A0 to reflect their roles. Also corrected the lb_8001204C prototype from an int first parameter to HSD_JObj*, then updated mnmain callers and headers accordingly.
what_changed: Changed lb_8001204C's declaration in lb_00F9.h from void lb_8001204C(int, HSD_JObj**, u16*, int) to void lb_8001204C(HSD_JObj*, HSD_JObj**, u16*, int), matching how callers pass root/cursor JObjs.
Changed mn_80229F60's second parameter type from int to HSD_JObj* in both mnmain.c and mnmain.h.

Changed mn_8022A440 from a two-argument int-based helper to void mn_8022A440(HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, s32), using the JObj argument as the root passed into lb_8001204C and the s32 argument for selected-state/index behavior.
Converted local stack arrays and temporaries that hold JObj pointers from int or int* to HSD_JObj* or HSD_JObj** equivalents in mn_8022A5D0 and fn_8022AFEC.
Replaced integer zero assignment for JObj pointer temporaries with NULL.

Simplified struct mn_80229A7C_t from individually named offsets and padding fields into u8 x0/x1/x2, HSD_JObj* x4[42], and HSD_Text* xAC, with comments identifying menu type, cursor/state fields, menu JObjs, and description text.
Updated uses of former struct fields x10 and x3C to index into the x4 JObj array, notably x4[3] and x4[14].
Renamed locals in mn_8022B3A0 for clarity, including temp_r27 to gobj, temp_r3 to root_jobj, temp_r3_4 to cursor_jobj, temp_r3_2 to menu_data, var_r18_2 to idx, and arg0 to state.
Added explanatory comments to mn_unk1 fields x2 and x4 in mnmain.h, identifying them as selected index and selected value.
smart_moves: Propagated the lb_8001204C type correction into both the library header and mnmain call sites, avoiding a local-only cleanup that would leave the shared prototype misleading.

Recognized that the apparent padded struct layout in mn_80229A7C_t was actually a dense array of 42 HSD_JObj* entries from offset x4 through xA8, with xAC as the following HSD_Text* description field.
Used array indexing into menu_data->x4 to replace fake named offset fields, which better matches the loop that fills all 0x2A JObj pointers with lb_80011E24.
Kept matching-sensitive stack behavior explicit with PAD_STACK, adjusting the padding in mn_8022A440 after the signature/local type cleanup.

Improved semantic readability without changing known symbol names, using role-based local names only where the diff evidence clearly showed object roles such as root_jobj, cursor_jobj, menu_data, and gobj.
decomp_lessons: When a helper walks or extracts JObjs from a hierarchy, verify whether int parameters and int arrays are actually HSD_JObj* values; correcting the shared prototype can unlock cleaner caller decompilation.
Large structs with repeated pointer-sized fields and loops over numeric indices may be better represented as arrays instead of padding plus guessed individual fields.
If code repeatedly calls lb_80011E24(root_jobj, &data->x4[idx], idx, -1) over a fixed count, that is strong evidence for a contiguous JObj pointer array in user data.
Use NULL for pointer temporaries after converting from integer decompiler output; it documents intent and helps maintain type consistency.
Renaming locals to semantic roles is useful in menu code once object lifetimes are evident: created GObj, loaded root JObj, loaded cursor JObj, and allocated user-data struct.
Header prototypes should be updated alongside source signatures to prevent stale int placeholders from spreading to future decomp work.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_JObj* rather than int for variables and parameters that hold joint-object pointers.
Use root_jobj for the top-level loaded menu joint from HSD_JObjLoadJoint.
Use cursor_jobj for the loaded MenMainCursor_Top joint object.
Use gobj for the HSD_GObj created by GObj_Create when no more specific object name is known.
Use menu_data for allocated mnmain GObj user data.
Use state as the parameter name when a u8 argument is stored into menu_data->x2 and controls initial menu animation state.
Preserve placeholder struct field names like x0, x1, x2, x4, and xAC when the exact semantic names are still uncertain, but add comments when the role is supported by usage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed decompiler placeholder ints to pointer types while preserving function structure and matching-sensitive stack padding.
Represented the JObj collection as HSD_JObj* x4[42] because the code fills indices 0 through 0x29 in a loop, replacing offset-specific fields such as x10 and x3C with x4[3] and x4[14].
Adjusted the mn_8022A440 call in fn_8022AFEC to pass both the selected HSD_JObj* and mn_804A04F0.x1 after the function signature was expanded.
Maintained explicit casts and u8 assignments for menu_data fields to avoid disturbing matching around narrow fields.
Kept PAD_STACK in functions where stack layout remains matching-sensitive, including a changed PAD_STACK size in mn_8022A440.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context. The dump reports one issue comment but no body excerpt was included, so no concrete reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Audit other callers of lb_8001204C to ensure they also use HSD_JObj* for the first argument and HSD_JObj** output arrays.
Investigate whether struct mn_80229A7C_t fields x0, x1, x2, x4, and xAC can be given stronger semantic names after more mnmain functions are decompiled.
Check whether menu_data->x4[3] and menu_data->x4[14] correspond to stable named menu components that deserve enum constants or documented indices.
Review adjacent mnmain menu functions for remaining int locals that are actually HSD_JObj*, HSD_Text*, or GObj user-data pointers.
